name: TAO 4h Deep Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a deeper pullback resets momentum without fully breaking medium-term structure, fast value is reclaimed, and continuation resumes
thesis: The cycle explicitly wants trend-reset continuation after a deeper pullback. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the only basket member with current trend-up contamination and enough amplitude for a meaningful second leg after reset. The edge is not shallow EMA-touch continuation; it is entering only after a genuine reset and reclaim.
invalidation_condition: If deeper pullbacks mostly represent broken trend rather than reset opportunity, or if reset-and-reclaim remains too sparse, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional move cools through a deeper pullback, fast structure is reclaimed from within medium-term support, and directional pressure returns for another leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Trend-contaminated but noisy assets often need larger resets before the next move can travel. TAO has enough amplitude that deeper resets can offer better continuation location than shallow pullbacks.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it has the strongest trend potential in the basket and enough move size for deeper-reset continuation to matter.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: required pullback depth and stop sizing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: What looks like a reset may actually be the start of structural breakdown, causing continuation entries to arrive into a broken market.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but continuation stalls too often, tighten the structure-hold condition around EMA_55 before changing pullback depth.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Fewer trades than mean reversion, but a higher share of meaningful second-leg continuations when the reset is genuine.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h shows non-sparse reset-and-reclaim trades and at least one cross-lane preserves the same continuation structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or repeated reset entries that fail before the first target.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but still denser than earlier TAO continuation families because the sequence is simplified to depth, reclaim, and pressure recovery

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for deeper reset continuation under current regime contamination.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reset-and-reclaim sequences with positive post-cost first-target behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h resets keep becoming broken trends or remain too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with larger continuation legs after deeper resets.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reset continuation survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves the same reset-reclaim pattern with smaller but cleaner moves.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the mechanism needs TAO amplitude and regime contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, cleaner reset continuation than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same reset continuation survives on a noisier transition lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 4h preserves enough reset-and-go structure to validate portability.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h turns the thesis into noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Messier but sharper continuation bursts than ETH.
